verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a lysosomal ABC transporter that localizes to the lysosomal membrane and functions in peptide translocation from the cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. The NCBI summary explicitly identifies it as a member of the MDR/TAP subfamily involved in antigen presentation and lysosomal peptide transport — a classic endomembrane resident. No documented PM pool, no TM-partner surface anchoring, no cell-state or tissue-restricted surface display, no ectodomain shedding, and no documented lysosomal exocytosis-dependent surface exposure. While it is a lysosomal TM protein, there is no evidence it reaches the outer leaflet via exocytosis or any other mechanism under baseline or induced conditions.
